_comment: Azure Policy: deny resources without required tags. Apply at subscription or management-group scope. The 'Modify' effect could auto-add tag with empty value, but for Day-N hygiene we use 'Deny' so the application team is forced to supply real values at deploy time. Mode 'Indexed' restricts to resource types that support tags.
name: require-cost-center-and-application-tags
displayName: Require cost-center-id and application-id tags on all resources
description: Denies creation of any resource that lacks both 'cost-center-id' and 'application-id' tags. Day-N: prevents drift in tag coverage that would defeat Cost Management allocation.

metadata.description: Tags whose presence is mandatory at create-time.

_implementation_note: An absent tag evaluates to null, NOT to empty string -- so 'equals ""' on a missing tag never fires. We use 'exists false' to catch missing, and 'notLike "?*"' (one-or-more-chars) to catch present-but-empty values. Together these reject both shapes of empty-tag, which is what 'real value required' means.
